title: Scout with Multiple Bee Stings — Anaphylaxis
briefing: 9-year-old boy on a Scout trip stung multiple times near a lake shore. Hives spreading rapidly over torso, voice starting to change, and he says his throat feels 'funny.' No known allergy history. This is a developing airway emergency. Nearest road is a 2-hour carry.
terrain: Forested lake shore, 2 hours from the nearest road; open meadow nearby usable as a landing zone
evacNote: Anaphylaxis — give epinephrine now, immediate evac, may need repeat dose

patient.mechanism: Multiple bee stings with systemic allergic reaction (anaphylaxis)

injuries.notes: Spreading hives, early voice change and throat tightness, hypotension and tachycardia. No prior known allergy. Airway involvement evolving.
